name: Virginia M. Kendall
current_employer: U.S. District Court, Northern District of Illinois
bio: Chief Judge Virginia M. Kendall has served on the U.S. District Court for the Northern District of Illinois since 2006, appointed by President George W. Bush, and became Chief Judge in 2024. Previously a federal prosecutor for over ten years, she served as Deputy Chief of the Criminal Division and Child Exploitation Coordinator. She teaches trial practice and federal litigation at the University of Chicago Law School, Northwestern, and Loyola, and is co-author of books on child exploitation and trafficking. Kendall lectures internationally in over 30 countries and serves on Judicial Conference committees. She received a JD from Loyola University Chicago School of Law, an MA from Northwestern University, and a BA from Northwestern University.

career.employer: U.S. District Court, Northern District of Illinois
U.S. District Court, Northern District of Illinois
U.S. Attorney's Office, Northern District of Illinois

education.school: Loyola University Chicago School of Law

name: Marvin Isgur
current_employer: U.S. Bankruptcy Court, Southern District of Texas
bio: Judge Marvin Isgur has served as a United States Bankruptcy Judge for the Southern District of Texas since 2004, reappointed in 2018 through 2032. He served as Chief Judge from 2009 to 2012 and was appointed by Chief Justice Roberts to the Judicial Conference Committee on Court Administration and Case Management—the sole bankruptcy judge on that committee. He has authored over 500 memorandum opinions. Before the bench, Isgur practiced representing debtors and trustees in Chapter 11, 7, and 9 cases and spent 12 years as an executive at a Houston real estate development company. He also serves as adjunct at the University of Houston Law Center. Isgur received a JD, with high honors, from the University of Houston Law Center, an MBA, with honors, from Stanford University, and a BS from the University of Houston.

name: Heather K. McShain
current_employer: U.S. District Court, Northern District of Illinois
bio: Hon. Heather K. McShain has served as a U.S. Magistrate Judge for the Northern District of Illinois since 2020. Previously, she served for ten years as an Assistant United States Attorney in the Northern District of Illinois, rising to Deputy Chief of the Financial Crimes Section and supervising the Health Care Fraud Unit. She also served as Deputy Chief of General Crimes and as a line AUSA in the General Crimes, Financial Crimes, and Public Corruption and Organized Crime Sections. From 2002 to 2010, McShain was an AUSA in the Civil Division of the U.S. Attorney's Office for the Southern District of New York. Earlier in her career, she was an associate at Jones Day in Washington, D.C., and clerked for Judge Robert Cowen of the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Third Circuit and Judge William Lee of the U.S. District Court for the Northern District of Indiana. McShain currently serves as Adjunct Professor of Law at Notre Dame Law School and presides over the Veterans Treatment Court. She received a JD from Notre Dame Law School and a BS in Mathematics from the University of Notre Dame.

career.employer: U.S. District Court, Northern District of Illinois

career.role: Assistant U.S. Attorney (Deputy Chief, Financial Crimes)
career.employer: U.S. Attorney's Office, Northern District of Illinois
U.S. Attorney's Office, Southern District of New York
U.S. Court of Appeals for the Third Circuit
U.S. District Court, Northern District of Indiana

name: Jesse G. Reyes
bio: Hon. Jesse G. Reyes is a Justice of the Illinois Appellate Court, First District since 2012, with over 41 years of legal experience. He has served as Presiding Justice of multiple divisions, Chair of the Executive Committee, and Co-Chair of the Diversity and Inclusion Committee. Previously a Judge on the Circuit Court of Cook County from 1997 to 2012 in the Chancery, Domestic Violence, and Municipal divisions, his earlier career included Senior Supervising Attorney at the City of Chicago Corporation Counsel's Office and Assistant Attorney at the Chicago Board of Education. Reyes received a JD from John Marshall Law School (now UIC School of Law) and a BA from the University of Illinois at Chicago.

career.employer: City of Chicago Corporation Counsel's Office

education.school: John Marshall Law School (now UIC School of Law)

name: Bruce Markell
bio: Hon. Bruce Markell is the Edward Avery Harriman Lecturer in Law and Professor of Bankruptcy Law and Practice at Northwestern Pritzker School of Law since 2015. He previously served as U.S. Bankruptcy Judge for the District of Nevada (2004–2013) and on the Bankruptcy Appellate Panel for the Ninth Circuit. Markell clerked for then-Judge Anthony M. Kennedy, was a partner at Sidley & Austin, and has held visiting professorships at Harvard Law School and Peking University. He served as primary drafter of Kosovo's bankruptcy law and is co-author of four law school casebooks. Markell received a JD from UC Davis School of Law, where he was editor-in-chief of the law review and received the School of Law Medal, and a BA from Pitzer College.

career.role: Law Clerk, Justice Anthony M. Kennedy
career.employer: U.S. Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit

name: Mark Shore
bio: Dr. Mark Shore is Director and Economist at CME Group, where he generates economic analysis on global financial markets, identifies emerging trends, and evaluates their impact on CME Group's asset classes. A recognized alternative investment expert with over 30 years of capital markets experience, he also serves as Adjunct Professor at DePaul University. Shore previously held roles as Executive Director of the Arditti Center for Risk Management at DePaul, COO of VK Capital (a Morgan Stanley CTA unit), and Head of Risk at Octane Research. He has authored over 80 articles and contributed chapters to Wiley & Sons publications on hedge funds and commodities. He holds a Series 3 license since 1992. Shore received a Doctor of Business Administration (DBA) from DePaul University, an MBA in Finance, Behavioral Science, and Econometrics from the University of Chicago, and a BS in Finance from DePaul University.

career.role: Executive Director, Arditti Center for Risk Management & Faculty

name: Benjamin Blum
bio: Benjamin Blum is a Managing Director at Flexpoint Ford, where he focuses on identifying, valuing, and executing investment opportunities in the Asset Opportunities strategy. Based in New York, he joined Flexpoint in 2016 as Vice President and was promoted to Managing Director in 2021. Prior to Flexpoint Ford, Blum spent nearly seven years at Morgan Stanley in the Fixed Income Division, progressing from Analyst to Vice President, with a focus on asset-based lending and securitized products. He has extensive experience evaluating, structuring, and managing term securitizations and warehouse credit lines across a broad range of asset classes. Blum received a BSBA in Finance, summa cum laude, from Washington University in St. Louis (Olin Business School).

education.degree: BSBA in Finance, summa cum laude
education.school: Washington University in St. Louis (Olin Business School)

name: Michele Gambera
current_title: Head of Strategic Asset Allocation Modeling
bio: Michele Gambera has over 25 years of top-down investment management experience, focusing on asset allocation, risk management, and portfolio construction. After two years at the Chicago Fed as economist, he was at Morningstar for ten years in several capacities including chief economist of Ibbotson Associates, and among other things, he is one of the two authors of the Morningstar Star Ratings methodology for mutual funds. He then was at UBS Asset Management for 15 years, leading the development of models for strategic and tactical asset allocation, as well as envisioning portfolio solutions for large complex institutional clients such as sovereign wealth funds. A graduate of the University of Trento in Italy, Michele holds a PhD from the Pennsylvania State University. A CFA charterholder and co-author of two chapters in the CFA curriculum, Michele has published his research in several peer-reviewed journals, including the Journal of Finance. He has taught economics and finance at the Illinois Institute of Technology, Loyola University Chicago, University of Illinois at Chicago, University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign, and University of West Virginia.

career.role: Head of Strategic Asset Allocation Modeling

name: Sarah Jacobson
bio: Sarah Jacobson is an Investment Manager and Legal Counsel at Omni Bridgeway, conducting due diligence on potential investments and advising on U.S. law matters. With over 17 years of legal experience, she previously served as Senior Litigation Associate at Haynes and Boone from 2010 to 2018 handling complex commercial litigation and as an associate at Willkie Farr & Gallagher. Jacobson has been an Adjunct Professor at Cardozo School of Law teaching legal writing since 2013 and was named a Thomson Reuters NY Super Lawyers Rising Star from 2013 to 2017. She received a JD from the University of Michigan Law School and a BA from Cornell University.

name: Marc Carmel
bio: Marc Carmel is Managing Member of McDonald Hopkins' Chicago office, where he co-leads the Private Equity and Litigation Finance practice groups. With two decades of experience, he represents public and private companies, PE firms, directors, and lenders in restructurings, M&A, and litigation finance. Previously at Kirkland & Ellis, Paul Hastings, and Longford Capital, where he served as Director leading bankruptcy and restructuring investments. Ranked Band 4 in Bankruptcy/Restructuring by Chambers USA 2025, Carmel also holds an inactive CPA credential. He received a JD from Harvard Law School, a Master of Accounting from the University of Michigan, and a BBA from the University of Michigan.

name: Steven Nachtwey
bio: Steven Nachtwey is a Partner at Bartlit Beck LLP specializing in complex commercial litigation, particularly residential mortgage-backed securities and financial services disputes. He represented MassMutual in RMBS litigation against Credit Suisse and Goldman Sachs, served as lead co-counsel for an ad hoc group of investors in a $950 million settlement involving Bank of America, and represented MGIC against Lehman Brothers in bankruptcy fraud litigation. Nachtwey received a JD, summa cum laude, from the University of Minnesota Law School and a BS, with distinction, from the University of Wisconsin-Madison.

name: Craig Martin
bio: Craig Martin is Chairman, Americas of Willkie Farr & Gallagher LLP and member of the firm's Executive Committee. He founded the Willkie Chicago office in 2020 and grew it to over 100 lawyers. Previously chairman of a national law firm, Martin is recognized as a "courtroom to boardroom" lawyer with approximately 50 trials and arbitrations across antitrust, employment, patent, ERISA, and commercial disputes. He is ranked in Chambers Global and USA in four practice areas and listed in Chicago Magazine's "Power 100" and Crain's "Who's Who." Martin received a JD from Harvard Law School and a BA, magna cum laude, from the University of Notre Dame.

name: Seth Deutsch
bio: Seth Deutsch is Founder of Samson Partners Group (Samson Operating Partners), providing executive advisory and board-level services to PE firms, family offices, and portfolio companies. A growth leader, operating partner, board member, and CEO, he has a track record of profitable growth for PE-backed, family-owned, and public companies. Previously President of AECOM's Industrial Group and CEO of its Facility Services Group, Deutsch currently serves as Board Member and Chief Growth Officer at E78 Partners. He received an MBA from the Kellogg School of Management at Northwestern University and an undergraduate degree from Rice University.

education.school: Kellogg School of Management, Northwestern University

name: Ed Gehres
bio: Ed Gehres is Managing Partner and co-founder of Invenio LLP, focused on litigation finance, complex litigation, and advisory services. With over 25 years of legal and leadership experience spanning BigLaw, in-house, and government, he previously served as General Counsel at 777 Partners overseeing 50-plus portfolio companies and as GC at Think Finance. Gehres was a partner at Squire Patton Boggs and Van Ness Feldman, and began his BigLaw career at Arnold & Porter. His early career included legislative and government affairs work. He received a JD from the University of Virginia School of Law, an MA in Issues and Crisis Management from George Washington University, and a BA in History from the University of Michigan.

education.school: University of Virginia School of Law

name: Kacey Wolmer
bio: Kacey Wolmer is Chief Operating Officer at Contingency Capital LLC, a litigation finance firm, where she has served since January 2021. With approximately 18 years of experience spanning finance and law, she previously served as Senior Vice President and Counsel at FirstKey Mortgage, a portfolio company of Cerberus Capital Management. Earlier in her career, Wolmer practiced as an associate at Hunton & Williams LLP specializing in structured finance and securitization, at Fortress Investment Group, at Morgan Lewis & Bockius LLP in private funds, and at Thacher Proffitt & Wood in structured finance. Wolmer received a JD from the University of Michigan Law School and a BA from Emory University.

name: Ken Epstein
bio: Ken Epstein is Co-Founder and Principal of Backlit Capital Solutions, a financial consulting and advisory firm specializing in legal finance and litigation risk management. With 25 years of experience in bankruptcy law, commercial litigation, restructuring, and finance, he previously served as Senior Investment Manager and Legal Counsel at Omni Bridgeway, leading the U.S. insolvency litigation finance platform. Before that, Epstein was Managing Director at MBIA and Associate Director at Standard & Poor's. He also teaches bankruptcy law as adjunct professor at Cardozo Law School. Epstein received a JD, cum laude, from Brooklyn Law School and an undergraduate accounting degree from the University of Maryland.

name: Matt Leland
bio: Matt Leland is Principal and Co-Founder of Backlit Capital Solutions, a legal finance consultancy. With over 20 years in commercial litigation and litigation finance, he previously served as Investment Manager and Legal Counsel at Omni Bridgeway and was a partner and commercial litigator for nearly two decades at King & Spalding and McDermott Will & Emery, recovering hundreds of millions in damages for clients. Leland's early career included service as a top aide to former U.S. Senator John Sununu. He is recognized in Best Lawyers in America, Legal 500, and Super Lawyers. Leland received a JD from Georgetown University Law Center and a BA in Political Science from the University of New Hampshire.

name: Jeffery Lula
bio: Jeffery Lula is a Managing Director at GLS Capital responsible for due diligence and monitoring of commercial litigation and arbitration investments. Previously a litigation partner at Kirkland & Ellis for nine years, he focused on complex commercial litigation including contract disputes, bankruptcy, corporate mismanagement, and insurance matters. Lula successfully argued before the Illinois Supreme Court and served as Articles Editor of the University of Chicago Law Review. He is a certified mediator by the Center for Conflict Resolution. Lula received a JD from the University of Chicago Law School (Mulroy Prize) and a BS in Psychology and English from the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign (Bronze Tablet, Highest Honors).

education.degree: BS in Psychology & English (Bronze Tablet, Highest Honors)

name: Joel Merkin
bio: Joel Merkin is a Managing Director at GLS Capital – one of the world’s largest private investment firms focused on litigation finance – where he is responsible for due diligence and monitoring of patent investments. Previously a Partner in the intellectual property litigation group at Kirkland & Ellis for over a decade, Joel is a registered patent attorney with experience litigating patent cases involving computer hardware and software, communication systems, consumer electronics, and medical devices. He received a JD from the University of Illinois College of Law and a BS in Computer Engineering from the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign.

education.school: University of Illinois College of Law

name: Eric Carlson
bio: Eric Carlson is a Managing Director at Burford Capital responsible for assessing and underwriting legal risk in Burford's patent group. With over 18 years in IP and litigation finance, he previously practiced as a patent litigator at Katten Muchin Rosenman and Jones Day, handling matters involving software, hardware, semiconductors, and smartphones. Carlson transitioned to litigation finance in 2014 as an analyst at Gerchen Keller Capital, which was later acquired by Burford. He is listed in IAM Strategy 300 and recognized by Chambers. Carlson received a JD from the University of Illinois College of Law and a BS in Computer Science from the University of Illinois College of Engineering.

education.school: University of Illinois College of Law
University of Illinois College of Engineering

name: Andrew Stulce
bio: Andrew Stulce is a Director at Longford Capital with over 15 years of legal experience. He previously litigated at McGuireWoods LLP and Hunton & Williams LLP, specializing in complex antitrust and insurance recovery, and clerked for Hon. Charles A. Pannell Jr. of the U.S. District Court for the Northern District of Georgia. At Longford, he assists with investment sourcing, due diligence, and portfolio monitoring. Stulce also serves as adjunct professor at UGA School of Law teaching litigation finance and sits on the Board of Directors of the Chicago Lawyers' Committee for Civil Rights. He is admitted in Illinois, Georgia, and Tennessee. Stulce received a JD, cum laude, from UGA School of Law and a BS in Business Administration and Romance Languages from UNC Chapel Hill.

career.role: Law Clerk, Hon. Charles A. Pannell Jr.
career.employer: U.S. District Court, Northern District of Georgia

education.degree: BS in Business Administration & Romance Languages

name: Jason Levine
bio: Jason Levine is a Partner at Foley & Lardner with 30 years of combined experience as a first-chair trial lawyer, antitrust litigator, and litigation finance executive. He previously launched and led the D.C. office of Omni Bridgeway, managing antitrust and commercial investments involving billion-dollar claims. Levine has first-chaired over a dozen trials and secured over $3 billion in relief for plaintiff-side corporate clients, in addition to saving defense-side clients from over $17 billion in potential liability exposure. He clerked for Judge Randall Rader of the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit and was formerly a partner at McDermott Will & Emery, Vinson & Elkins, and Alston & Bird. Levine received a JD, cum laude, from Harvard Law School and a BA, summa cum laude (Phi Beta Kappa), from Brandeis University.

career.employer: U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit

education.degree: BA, summa cum laude (Phi Beta Kappa)

name: Ben Whiting
bio: Ben Whiting is a Senior Partner at Keller Postman specializing in complex litigation including consumer protection, labor and employment, and product liability in class action and mass tort contexts. Previously a partner at Bartlit Beck LLP for a decade, he led a $60 million jury verdict against infant formula manufacturers in 2024 and developed Keller Postman's mass arbitration strategy. Named a 2022 Plaintiffs' Lawyers Trailblazer by the National Law Journal. Before law school, Whiting taught philosophy and logic at multiple universities. He received a JD, with High Honors, from the University of Chicago Law School, an MA in Philosophy from the University of Wyoming, and a BS in Finance and Philosophy from Trinity University.

name: Will Marra
bio: Will Marra is a Director at Certum Group and Lecturer in Law at UPenn Carey Law School, where he co-teaches litigation finance. He formerly clerked for Justice Samuel A. Alito Jr. of the U.S. Supreme Court and Chief Judge William H. Pryor Jr. of the Eleventh Circuit. Marra previously litigated at Cooper & Kirk PLLC and consulted at Bain & Company. In litigation finance since 2019, he is a published scholar on agency costs in third-party litigation finance and serves as a board member of the International Legal Finance Association. Marra received a JD, magna cum laude, from Harvard Law School and a BA, magna cum laude, from Harvard College.

career.role: Law Clerk, Justice Samuel A. Alito Jr.
career.employer: Supreme Court of the United States

career.role: Law Clerk, Chief Judge William H. Pryor Jr.
career.employer: U.S. Court of Appeals for the Eleventh Circuit

name: William Henderson
current_employer: Indiana University Maurer School of Law
bio: William Henderson holds the Stephen F. Burns Chair on the Legal Profession at Indiana University Maurer School of Law, where he has taught since 2003. He is founding director of the Milt and Judi Stewart Center on the Global Legal Profession and co-founder of the Institute for the Future of Law Practice. Named to the NLJ's 100 Most Influential Lawyers in America and Most Influential Person in Legal Education by The National Jurist in 2015 and 2016, his research focuses on empirical analysis of the legal profession. Henderson received a JD, with honors, from the University of Chicago and a BA, magna cum laude, in History and Economics from Case Western Reserve University.

career.role: Stephen F. Burns Chair on the Legal Profession
career.employer: Indiana University Maurer School of Law
Indiana University Maurer School of Law
Indiana University Maurer School of Law

education.degree: BA, magna cum laude, in History & Economics

name: Leonard Brahin
bio: Leonard Brahin is an Associate in Holland & Knight's Chicago office focusing on litigation and dispute resolution, lawyer ethics, and legal services transactions. He handles expedited matters involving TROs and preliminary injunctions in high-stakes business disputes and develops legal frameworks for law firm management services organizations. During law school, Brahin externed for Hon. Thomas M. Durkin of the Northern District of Illinois and assisted with appeals for Hon. John Z. Lee of the Seventh Circuit. Prior to law school, he worked at a transportation logistics company in Chicago. Brahin received a JD from DePaul Law School.

career.employer: U.S. District Court, Northern District of Illinois
U.S. Court of Appeals for the Seventh Circuit

name: J. Samuel Tenenbaum
bio: J. Samuel Tenenbaum is Clinical Professor of Law and Director of the Complex Civil Litigation and Investor Protection Center at Northwestern Pritzker School of Law. A litigation expert who has practiced in courts throughout the U.S. and in foreign jurisdictions, he clerked for U.S. District Judge Richard W. McLaren in the Northern District of Illinois. Tenenbaum is a member of Tenenbaum Law LLC, a Commissioner on the Uniform Laws Commission, and served as Editor-in-Chief of the Northwestern Law Review. He has been a visiting professor at Tel Aviv University, Hebrew University, and IDC Herzliya. Tenenbaum received a JD, cum laude (Order of the Coif), from Northwestern University and a BA from Ohio State University.

career.role: Clinical Professor & Director, Complex Civil Litigation Center
Law Clerk, Judge Richard W. McLaren
career.employer: U.S. District Court, Northern District of Illinois

education.degree: JD, cum laude (Order of the Coif)

name: Matt Oxman
bio: Matt Oxman is Vice President of Underwriting at Bench Walk Advisors since 2023, assisting with case review and investment analysis. Previously VP of Business Development at LexShares from 2017 to 2023, he expanded the firm's caseload and established relationships in litigation funding. Oxman's earlier career included work as a litigation associate at Dewey & LeBoeuf specializing in antitrust, securities, FCPA, and white-collar defense, and as an MBA Summer Associate at J.P. Morgan Private Bank's Law Firms Group. He received an MBA from NYU Stern School of Business, a JD from Columbia Law School, and an AB, magna cum laude, in Politics from Princeton University.

education.degree: AB, magna cum laude, in Politics

name: Victoria Sahani
bio: Victoria Sahani is a Professor of Law at Boston University School of Law and an internationally recognized scholar in arbitration law and third-party litigation funding. With 18 years of international arbitration experience and over 16 years of funding expertise, she co-authored Third-Party Funding in International Arbitration (Wolters Kluwer)—the first book on this topic. Previously Associate Provost for Community and Inclusion at BU, she held prior academic posts at Arizona State (as Associate Dean) and Washington & Lee. Sahani serves as independent arbitrator, consultant, and expert witness, and is a member of the ALI and an American Bar Foundation Fellow. She received a JD from Harvard Law School and an AB in Psychology from Harvard College.

name: Erika Levin
bio: Erika Levin is a Partner at Fox Rothschild leading the firm's Litigation Finance team, focusing on international disputes and serving as both counsel and arbitrator/mediator. She previously served as Senior VP and General Counsel at The Judge (litigation finance), was a partner at Lewis Baach Kaufmann Middlemiss, and a partner at Stone & Magnanini (formerly Boies Schiller NJ office). Levin clerked for judges on the NJ Appellate Division, Third Circuit, and D.N.J., and has served as adjunct professor at Rutgers Law since 2004. She also worked at the U.S. Embassy in Malta on EU accession. Levin received a JD from Rutgers Law School and a BA from Rutgers University.

career.employer: U.S. Court of Appeals for the Third Circuit

name: Josh Gammon
current_employer: WealthDocket / Northwestern Pritzker School of Law
bio: Josh Gammon is Co-Founder and Managing Member of WealthDocket Advisors, a litigation funding advisory and IP asset management firm that created the world's first crowdfunded IP monetization platform. He has served as Adjunct Instructor at Northwestern Pritzker School of Law teaching IP Valuation since 2018. With 15 years of IP experience, Gammon previously held roles as Managing Director at Hilco IP Merchant Banking and Director at Ocean Tomo. He is licensed in Illinois and holds a Series 7 credential. Gammon received a JD from Washington University in St. Louis School of Law and a BS in Finance, with high distinction, from Indiana University's Kelley School of Business.

education.school: Washington University in St. Louis School of Law

education.degree: BS in Finance, with high distinction
education.school: Indiana University, Kelley School of Business

name: Ryan N. Phelan
bio: Ryan N. Phelan is a Partner at Marshall, Gerstein & Borun, a Chicago-based IP law firm. With over 16 years of experience in patent prosecution, litigation, IP opinions, and post-grant proceedings, he specializes in AI, machine learning, medical devices, blockchain, fintech, and emerging technologies. Previously a technology consultant at Accenture for Fortune 500 clients, Phelan also serves as Adjunct Professor at Northwestern Pritzker School of Law teaching Patenting Software Inventions and is founder of the PatentNext legal blog. He received a JD from Northwestern University Pritzker School of Law and an MBA from Northwestern University's Kellogg School of Management.

education.school: Northwestern University Pritzker School of Law
Northwestern University, Kellogg School of Management

name: Ryan Schultz
bio: Ryan Schultz is Head of Business Development at Pine Valley Capital Partners, a litigation finance firm providing capital solutions for plaintiff trial law firms and legal asset owners. Previously VP at TT Capital Partners in healthcare investments, VP of Business Development at LifeFlow Partners, and VP of Corporate Development at Bridgepointe Technologies, he brings a background in investment banking and financial modeling. Schultz received a BA in Finance and Accounting from the University of Denver's Daniels College of Business.

education.school: University of Denver, Daniels College of Business

name: Justin Maleson
bio: Justin Maleson is a Managing Director and Assistant General Counsel at Victory Park Capital, co-heading the firm's legal credit investment strategy. He is responsible for sourcing, analyzing, executing, and managing investments in the legal industry. Previously a Director at Longford Capital from 2018 to 2024, where he originated litigation finance opportunities, Maleson was a litigation partner at Jenner & Block for a decade advising on complex commercial and IP matters. He received a JD, with highest honors, from the University of New Hampshire and a BS from the University of Maryland Smith School of Business.

education.school: University of Maryland Smith School of Business

name: Joshua Libling
bio: Joshua Libling is Co-Founder and Managing Director of Arcadia Finance, a litigation funding firm launched in 2024 with fellow former Validity Finance executives. He focuses on operations, financial analytics, and translating legal merits into risk data. Previously at Validity Finance as Portfolio Counsel and Director of Risk Analytics, Libling spent a decade as associate and counsel at Boies Schiller & Flexner specializing in antitrust, securities, and class actions. He clerked for Hon. Kenneth Karas (S.D.N.Y.) and Hon. Chester Straub (Second Circuit) and is recognized among Lawdragon's Global 100 in Legal Finance. Libling received a JD, magna cum laude (Order of the Coif), from NYU Law School and a BA from the University of Chicago.

career.employer: U.S. Court of Appeals for the Second Circuit
U.S. District Court, Southern District of New York

education.degree: JD, magna cum laude (Order of the Coif)

name: Patrick Dempsey
bio: Patrick Dempsey is Director of Commercial Litigation Strategy at Certum Group since 2025, overseeing origination, structuring, and monitoring of commercial litigation financing and portfolio solutions. Previously a Director at Burford Capital and CIO of U.S. operations at Therium Capital Management, his earlier career included roles as litigator at Proskauer and senior associate at Hogan Lovells. Recognized by Lawdragon, Who's Who Legal, and Leaders League for litigation finance expertise. Dempsey received a JD, magna cum laude (Order of the Coif, Managing Editor of the Tulane Law Review), from Tulane University Law School and a BS from the University of New Orleans.

education.degree: JD, magna cum laude (Order of the Coif, Managing Editor Tulane Law Review)

name: Jack Kelly
bio: Jack Kelly is Managing Director of the American Legal Finance Association (ALFA), the leading trade association for consumer litigation funding companies, and Partner in the McPherson Group LLP advising on public policy risk management. With two decades of experience in structured finance and tax-related issues, he also serves as adjunct professor at Fordham School of Law teaching compliance and crisis management. Kelly previously served as aide to two U.S. Presidents and held Congressional staff positions. A 28-year U.S. Army veteran in active and reserve service, he commanded a Special Forces unit and received the Legion of Merit.

name: Eric Schuller
current_employer: Alliance for Responsible Consumer Legal Funding (ARC)
bio: Eric Schuller is President of the Alliance for Responsible Consumer Legal Funding (ARC) since 2011, a national trade association representing consumer legal funding companies. A government affairs expert with 50 years of professional experience, he previously served as Senior Policy Advisor in the Illinois Lieutenant Governor's Office. Schuller chairs the Public Outreach Committee of the State Government Affairs Council and serves on the Advisory Board of NYU School of Law's Dispute Financing Library. He is a U.S. Army and Illinois National Guard veteran who served from 1979 to 2001.

career.employer: Alliance for Responsible Consumer Legal Funding (ARC)

name: Paul Kong
bio: Paul Kong is Executive Director and President of the International Legal Finance Association (ILFA), the global trade association representing the commercial legal finance industry. Based in Washington, D.C., ILFA engages, educates, and influences the legislative, regulatory, and judicial landscapes as the voice of the commercial legal finance sector.

name: Albert Chang
current_employer: Northwestern Pritzker School of Law / Fannie Mae
bio: Albert Chang is a Senior Director at Fannie Mae and Adjunct Professor at Northwestern Pritzker School of Law, where he teaches Fintech Law and Regulation. Previously Senior Counsel at the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) in the Office of Competition and Innovation and Office of Fair Lending, his earlier career included roles at American Express in Fair Lending and as Senior Consultant at EY in Quantitative Economics and Risk Management. Chang received a JD, cum laude (Dean's Merit Scholarship), from Yeshiva University and a BA in Physics and Economics (Philip Evans Scholar) from Swarthmore College.

education.degree: BA in Physics & Economics (Philip Evans Scholar)

name: Charles Agee
bio: Charles Agee is CEO and Founder of Westfleet Advisors, a litigation finance advisory firm based in Nashville. With over 27 years in litigation finance, he co-founded Augusta Capital—one of the first litigation finance companies—in 1998 during his senior year of college, serving as EVP and COO for 15 years and overseeing financing in over a thousand complex matters. Westfleet provides transaction advisory, competitive process management, and expert valuations for the litigation finance industry. Agee received a BA in Economics (Phi Beta Kappa) from Rhodes College.

name: Michael Kelley
bio: Michael Kelley is a Partner at Parker Poe Adams & Bernstein and DC Office Development Partner. He lectures at the University of Chicago Law School teaching litigation finance and is ranked Chambers Band 1 USA in Litigation Finance Deal Counsel. Named among Lawdragon's Global 100 Leaders in Legal Finance as a top-ten global advisor, Kelley brings over 34 years of experience. He previously served as General Counsel of EMP Global, overseeing approximately $8 billion in assets across 10 investment funds in emerging markets, and serves on the Advisory Committee of the UChicago Law School Center on Law and Finance.

career.employer: Various (Latin America, Asia, Europe, Middle East)

name: Anthony Sebok
current_employer: Benjamin N. Cardozo School of Law / NYU
bio: Anthony Sebok holds the Joseph and Sadie Danciger Chair in Law at Cardozo School of Law and is Co-Director of the Jacob Burns Center for Ethics in the Practice of Law. An Adjunct Professor at NYU School of Law, he is a leading scholar in tort law, professional responsibility, and litigation finance. Sebok joined Cardozo in 2007 after serving as Centennial Professor at Brooklyn Law School for 15 years. He is Ethics Consultant to Burford Capital, a member of the ALI, and clerked for Chief Judge Edward Cahn of the Eastern District of Pennsylvania. He has held visiting professorships at Cornell, Tsinghua, Free University Berlin, and Princeton. Sebok received a PhD in Politics from Princeton University, a JD from Yale Law School, an MPhil from Oxford University, and a BA from Cornell University.

career.role: Law Clerk, Chief Judge Edward Cahn
career.employer: U.S. District Court, Eastern District of Pennsylvania

name: Suneal Bedi
current_employer: Indiana University Kelley School of Business
bio: Suneal Bedi is an Associate Professor and Jerome Bess Faculty Fellow at Indiana University's Kelley School of Business and Research Director of Business Ethics at the Institute of Corporate Governance and Ethics. His expertise spans intellectual property, marketing ethics, brand strategy, and business ethics. Previously a corporate associate at Ropes & Gray in real estate and finance, Bedi has published in numerous law reviews and business journals and writes for the New York Times, Forbes, and Washington Post. He received a PhD in Marketing and a PhD in Business Ethics (joint) and an MS in Marketing from the Wharton School at the University of Pennsylvania, a JD from Harvard Law School, and a BA, with Honors, in Economics and Russian Literature from Swarthmore College.

career.role: Associate Professor & Jerome Bess Faculty Fellow
career.employer: Indiana University, Kelley School of Business

education.degree: PhD in Marketing & PhD in Business Ethics (joint)
BA, with Honors, in Economics & Russian Literature

name: Daniel Bond
bio: Daniel Bond is Managing Director of Litica North America since 2024, overseeing U.S. operations. He started his career at Kirkland & Ellis as an associate and made partner in 2014, specializing in complex commercial and IP litigation. Bond transitioned to contingent risk and litigation funding as Managing Director and Global Director of Underwriting at Delta Capital Partners Management from 2019 to 2022, then served as EVP of Contingent Risk at DUAL North America. At Litica, he builds solutions for funders, credit funds, and law firms through bespoke insurance structures. He is one of few underwriters to have served senior roles on all sides of the market. Bond received a BA from Middle Tennessee State University.

career.role: Managing Director & Global Director of Underwriting

name: Matthew Blumenstein
bio: Matthew Blumenstein is Director, Head of Underwriting, and Deputy General Counsel at Statera Capital since 2020. He previously practiced general civil litigation for approximately ten years at Williams & Connolly LLP and clerked for Judge Karen LeCraft Henderson on the U.S. Court of Appeals for the D.C. Circuit. Blumenstein serves as Adjunct Professor at Vanderbilt Law School teaching commercial litigation finance. Named to Lawdragon's Global 100 Leaders in Litigation Finance in 2022 and a Super Lawyers Rising Star from 2014 to 2019. He received a JD from Vanderbilt Law School (Order of the Coif, Cecil D. Branstetter Award) and a BA from Stanford University.

career.role: Law Clerk, Judge Karen LeCraft Henderson
career.employer: U.S. Court of Appeals for the D.C. Circuit

education.degree: JD (Order of the Coif, Cecil D. Branstetter Award)

name: Jackson Schaap
bio: Jackson Schaap is Vice President and Partner at Signal Peak Partners, a litigation finance company launched in 2025 offering customized financing and private credit solutions for commercial and patent litigation. He previously spent seven years at Siltstone Capital, a multi-strategy alternative investment firm, where he led acquisitions and divestiture underwriting, valuation, and strategic planning across over $300 million in assets. At Rice University, Schaap was a four-year rowing team member and two-season captain. He received a BA in Mathematical Economic Analysis from Rice University.

name: Max Bernstein
bio: Max Bernstein is Vice President of Contingent Risk at CAC Specialty, procuring insurance solutions for contingent legal assets and liabilities. He previously litigated at Quinn Emanuel Urquhart & Sullivan and BraunHagey & Borden, and clerked for Hon. Cathy Seibel (S.D.N.Y.) and Hon. Denny Chin (Second Circuit). Bernstein served as Editor-in-Chief of the Fordham Law Review and is an Adjunct Professor at Fordham Law School. CAC's Contingent Risk Practice has placed over $4.5 billion in insurance coverage. He received a JD from Fordham University School of Law (Fordham Law School Prize, Mary Daly Scholar).

career.employer: U.S. Court of Appeals for the Second Circuit
U.S. District Court, Southern District of New York

education.degree: JD (Fordham Law School Prize, Mary Daly Scholar)

name: Kelly Daley
bio: Kelly Daley is Founder of Celsia Capital, a litigation finance platform. With over 18 years of experience, she previously served as Managing Director at Gerchen Capital Partners from 2024 to 2025, leading corporate monetization transactions, and spent seven years at Burford Capital from 2017 to 2024, rising from VP to MD and leading U.S. commercial underwriting for corporate finance secured by contingent legal assets. Daley's earlier career included roles as senior litigator at Orrick Herrington & Sutcliffe and associate at MoloLamken and McDermott Will & Emery. She clerked for Hon. D. Michael Fisher on the Third Circuit. Daley received a JD from Georgetown University and a BA in Literature and History from Georgetown University.

career.role: Law Clerk, Hon. D. Michael Fisher
career.employer: U.S. Court of Appeals for the Third Circuit

name: Adam Levitt
bio: Adam Levitt is Founding Partner of DiCello Levitt LLP, one of the nation's leading plaintiffs' advocates in complex, multidistrict, class action, and mass tort litigation. He has delivered over $25 billion in recoveries for clients, including serving as co-lead counsel in the three largest biotechnology class actions in U.S. history with $1.7 billion recovered. Levitt helped secure the $16 billion VW emissions settlement, won a $102.6 million GM jury verdict, and represents State AGs in PFAS cases. A monthly columnist for the National Law Journal, he previously practiced at Wolf Haldenstein and Grant & Eisenhofer. Levitt received a JD from Northwestern University Pritzker School of Law and an AB, magna cum laude, in Political Science from Columbia University.

education.school: Northwestern University Pritzker School of Law

education.degree: AB, magna cum laude, in Political Science

name: Alex Sha
bio: Alex Sha is Co-Founder and Chief Product and Technology Officer at Bridge Legal, an AI-powered legal technology company. A product leader rooted in innovation through data and technology, he previously served as Director of Product and Engineering at UpRight Law. Sha's earlier experience includes roles at Signal, Wolfram Alpha, and Blocher Consulting. Named a Rising Star in Tech by Crain's Chicago Business in 2020, he is currently pursuing an Executive MBA at the University of Chicago Booth School of Business. Sha received a BS in Computer Science and Business from the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign.

education.school: University of Chicago Booth School of Business

name: Daniel Wolfe
bio: Daniel Wolfe is Senior Director of Jury Consulting at Magna Legal Services with over 35 years of research experience and work on more than 1,800 cases. He is an expert in witness preparation, voir dire, jury selection, focus groups, and mock trials across antitrust, product liability, IP, and securities matters. Past President of the American Society of Trial Consultants, Wolfe previously held senior roles at DecisionQuest, TrialGraphix, FTI Consulting, and Litigation Sciences. He is a member of the APA and ABA. Wolfe received a JD, a PhD in Law and Psychology, and an MA in Psychology from the University of Nebraska-Lincoln, and a BS in Psychology and Sociology from Colorado State University.

name: David Jang
bio: David Jang is a Portfolio Manager at Legalist, an institutional alternative asset management firm with $1.6 billion AUM focused on legal assets and litigation finance. Previously a Principal at Bardin Hill Investment Partners and Dana Cove LRC, his earlier career included work as a structured credit analyst at Och-Ziff Capital Management. Jang practiced law as an associate at Wachtell, Lipton, Rosen & Katz and MoloLamken, and clerked for Hon. Sandra L. Lynch on the First Circuit. He received a JD from Stanford University Law School and a BA from Stanford University.

career.role: Law Clerk, Hon. Sandra L. Lynch
career.employer: U.S. Court of Appeals for the First Circuit

name: Daniel Linna
current_title: Senior Lecturer & Director of Law and Technology Initiatives
bio: Daniel Linna is Senior Lecturer and Director of Law and Technology Initiatives at Northwestern Pritzker School of Law, with a joint appointment at McCormick School of Engineering. He is affiliated faculty at Stanford CodeX—The Stanford Center for Legal Informatics—and teaches AI and Legal Reasoning, Law of AI and Robotics, and Innovation Lab. Previously a professor and founder of LegalRnD at Michigan State University College of Law, Linna practiced as equity partner at Honigman Miller. Named an ABA Journal Legal Rebel in 2018 and Fastcase 50 in 2015, he is a Fellow of the College of Law Practice Management. Linna received a JD, magna cum laude, from the University of Michigan Law School, an MA in Public Policy and Administration from Michigan State University, and a BA from the University of Michigan.

career.role: Senior Lecturer & Director of Law and Technology Initiatives

career.employer: Michigan State University College of Law

career.role: Law Clerk, Judge James L. Ryan

name: Mark York
bio: Mark York is Editor in Chief of LegalCast and Mass Tort News, providing real-time reporting on mass torts, complex litigation, regulatory oversight, and JPML/MDL coverage. He co-hosts the Mass Tort News LegalCast podcast and also serves as Consultant for Business Development at IP SecureTech LLC. With over 26 years of experience in legal services, York previously worked as a Litigation Analyst at Schiff Hardin and Wildman Harrold, focusing on intellectual property, antitrust, and complex insurance matters. He also hosts the GW Law Bench-Bar Conference.

name: Chris Dore
bio:
Chris Dore is Chief Strategy Officer at Bridge Legal, an AI-enabled legal fintech platform headquartered in Chicago that delivers end-to-end workflow infrastructure for law firms and litigation funders across the full case lifecycle. Elevated to CSO in January 2026 after joining as Managing Director of Strategic Opportunities in September 2024, Dore leads enterprise-wide strategy, investment initiatives, and portfolio management as Bridge Legal continues to scale its capital markets presence in high-volume consumer litigation.

Dore brings more than fifteen years of combined experience as a plaintiff's litigator and litigation finance professional. He was a Partner at Edelson PC, one of the nation's premier mass tort and class action firms, where he built deep expertise in complex multi-district litigation. He subsequently served as a Director at Burford Capital, the world's largest litigation funder, where he oversaw mass tort and single-event investments.

At Bridge Legal, Dore has been instrumental in sharpening the company's strategic focus, strengthening relationships across the funder and law firm ecosystem, and positioning the platform—anchored by its AI-driven Bridgify software—for its next phase of intentional, disciplined growth. His rare vantage point spanning the plaintiff's bar, institutional litigation finance, and legal technology makes him a distinctive voice on the convergence of capital, AI, and legal practice.

career.role: Director, Mass Tort & Single Event Investments
